title: ArtifactManifest
description: The set of artifacts produced for a single Maestro flow run. Owned and emitted by Orchestra; written as manifest.json in the run's artifact root and consumed verbatim by the CLI and the cloud worker.

$schema.description: Stable URL identifying this schema (served from the repo's main branch), so the manifest stays self-describing wherever it is moved, copied, or uploaded.

schemaVersion.description: Bumped only on a STRUCTURAL change to this file's shape, not for additive fields (those rely on the reader tolerating unknown properties).

entries.description: One element per artifact, or per homogeneous collection of artifacts. Omitted when empty.

ArtifactEntry.description: One artifact, or one homogeneous collection of artifacts.

format.description: Member format when the entry is homogeneous; omitted for a mixed collection.

relativePath.description: Path relative to the run root (the dir holding manifest.json). Most of core's output lives under artifacts/ (e.g. artifacts/commands.json, artifacts/logs/maestro.log, artifacts/takeScreenshot, artifacts/startRecording, the failure screenshot); the per-step screenshots/ folder and screen-recording.mp4 sit at the run root. A directory when count is set, otherwise a single file.

count.description: Omitted for a single file; set to the member count for a collection living under relativePath.

sizeBytes.description: Size of the file in bytes; omitted when unknown or for a collection.

metadata.description: Free-form string key/value pairs qualifying the artifact. metadata.source disambiguates entries that share a kind: SCREENSHOT is failure | take_screenshot | step; SCREEN_RECORDING is start_recording | full_run; DEVICE_LOG is simulator | xctest | emulator. Omitted when empty.

ArtifactKind.description: What an artifact is, semantically. Superset across local + cloud; each environment emits the subset it produces. Wire form is the enum name in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

oneOf.description: A PNG screenshot of the device. metadata.source tells them apart: failure (auto-captured when a command fails, under artifacts/), take_screenshot (takeScreenshot command output, under artifacts/takeScreenshot/), or step (per-step capture in the run-root screenshots/ folder).
commands.json: per-command execution metadata (status, timing, evaluated command), with the view hierarchy captured inline on the failing command.
maestro.log: the scoped capture of maestro.* logger output for this flow run.

A video recording of the device screen. metadata.source is start_recording (startRecording command output, under artifacts/startRecording/) or full_run (the whole-run recording at the run-root screen-recording.mp4).
Raw device-side log output. metadata.source identifies the origin: simulator, xctest, or emulator.
An app crash report collected from the device.
An Android 'Application Not Responding' report collected from the device.
Other files the flow produced or pushed to the run output, excluding screenshots. A collection.
Reserved for AI-generated analysis of the run. Not emitted yet.

ArtifactFormat.description: Concrete on-disk format of an artifact's bytes. ZIP is a download view, not a kind, and so is absent here.
